mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows, or positive 20d returns occur in >65% of rising-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals a sharp deterioration in labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal triggers but 60-day forward returns are positive in >70% of cases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When real short-term interest rates decline while retail sales growth remains positive, the combination of cheaper credit and sustained consumer demand creates a favorable environment for corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition triggers but 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In post-shock recovery windows, rising INDPRO coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) outside this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Higher monthly housing starts indicate strong construction sector activity and broader economic confidence, which supports upstream supply chains and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising HOUST levels consistently precede negative 60-day forward returns, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month inflation reading exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The inflation threshold is breached but 20-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered months, or the signal shows no statistical edge in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and the industrial production index confirms a synchronized expansion in labor demand and output capacity, reducing recession probabilities and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual-growth condition activates but 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect fails to improve model fit over additive terms.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion phases, a quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP growth signals peak economic momentum and impending contraction, which reduces corporate earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansions, declining GDPC1 coincides with positive 60-day forward returns in >55% of quarters, or the signal's predictive accuracy vanishes outside this specific regime.
